name: isis (fellow practitioner of magic)
shortDescription: Isis is a major goddess in ancient Egyptian religion, known for her magic, motherhood, and protection. She was a powerful sorceress and a devoted wife and mother.
description: Isis is one of the most important and enduring deities of ancient Egypt. Originally a relatively obscure goddess, she rose to prominence over time, eventually becoming one of the most widely worshipped deities in Egypt and the Greco-Roman world. Isis is primarily known as a goddess of magic, motherhood, healing, and protection. She is the devoted wife of Osiris and the mother of Horus. The myths surrounding Isis are central to Egyptian beliefs about death, resurrection, and the afterlife. One of her most famous stories involves her search for the dismembered body of Osiris, whom she resurrected using her magical abilities. She then conceived Horus, whom she protected from Set, Osiris's jealous brother and murderer. Isis's magical powers were legendary; she was said to have learned the secret name of Ra, giving her immense power. Her role as a mother goddess and protector made her particularly popular among women. The ankh, a symbol of life, is often associated with Isis, as she represents regeneration and rebirth. Her cult spread far beyond Egypt, reaching Greece and Rome, where she was syncretized with other goddesses and worshipped as a universal deity. She is a powerful figure representing feminine strength, magical prowess, and unwavering dedication.

sources.title: Egyptian Ideas of the Future Life
sources.author: E.A. Wallis Budge

sources.title: Myths and Symbols of Ancient Egypt
sources.author: Lucia Gahlin
